features.description: Two-phase scraper: list endpoint for IDs + individual endpoint for full notice details
done_criteria: Phase 1: fetches all notice IDs from GET /notices/v1/red (paginated, max 160/page)
Phase 2: for each notice ID, fetches full detail from GET /notices/v1/red/{id}
Jitter delay (1-3.5s) applied between BOTH list pages AND individual detail calls

Full detail payload includes: forename, name, date_of_birth, place_of_birth, sex_id, height, weight, nationalities, languages_spoken_ids, eyes_colors_id, hairs_id, distinguishing_marks, arrest_warrants (each with charge + issuing_country_id)
Scrape interval read from SCRAPE_INTERVAL_SECONDS env var
Retry mechanism (max 3 retries) on HTTP errors for both phases
If all retries fail for an individual notice, it is skipped and logged — not fatal
A single RabbitMQ message published per notice with full enriched payload

features.description: RabbitMQ producer — publishes enriched notice payloads to the queue
done_criteria: RabbitMQ connection created from RABBITMQ_URL env var
Each message contains the full enriched notice payload as JSON
Message includes: notice_id, fetched_at, forename, name, date_of_birth, place_of_birth, sex_id, height, weight, nationalities, languages, arrest_warrants list
Queue name read from RABBITMQ_QUEUE env var
Reconnect mechanism on connection drop
Dead-letter queue declared with x-dead-letter-routing-key

features.description: RabbitMQ consumer — reads enriched messages and persists to DB
done_criteria: Consumer continuously listens to the queue
On message receipt, full notice data is upserted into PostgreSQL
arrest_warrants stored as JSONB column
is_alarm flag set only when meaningful fields change (name, nationalities, arrest_warrants) — not image_url
Failed messages sent to dead letter queue (basic_nack requeue=False)
Every operation is logged with notice_id

features.description: Enhanced database model with full notice detail fields
done_criteria: RedNotice model includes all fields: notice_id (PK), forename, name, date_of_birth, place_of_birth, sex_id, height, weight, nationalities (TEXT[]), languages (TEXT[]), eyes_colors_id, hairs_id, distinguishing_marks, arrest_warrants (JSONB), image_url, created_at, updated_at, received_at, is_alarm
All fields from external API are nullable — no NOT NULL except notice_id, name, created_at, updated_at, is_alarm
New records can be inserted
Existing records can be updated (updated_at changes, is_alarm set when meaningful fields changed)
get_filter_options() returns distinct nationalities and issuing countries for UI dropdowns
get_all_notices() supports filter params: nationality, sex_id, issuing_country, charges_keyword, is_alarm_only
GET / returns the HTML UI
GET /api/notices returns JSON list, supports query params: nationality, sex, issuing_country, charges, is_alarm, limit
GET /api/notices/<path:notice_id> returns single record (path: converter for YYYY/NNNNN IDs)
GET /api/filters returns available filter options: {nationalities, issuing_countries, sex_options}
GET /api/thumbnail/<path:notice_id> proxies MinIO thumbnail — never exposes minio:9000 URL to browser
GET /api/stream SSE endpoint for live updates
GET /health liveness check
Port from FLASK_PORT env var

features.description: Rich HTML UI with multi-field filtering, sorting, and detailed notice cards
done_criteria: Filter panel: text search (name), nationality multi-select with flag emojis, gender selector (M/F/All), issuing country multi-select, charges keyword input, alarms-only toggle
Filter dropdowns populated dynamically from GET /api/filters on page load
Filtering sends requests to GET /api/notices with query params (server-side filtering)
Sort options: newest first, name A-Z, nationality
Each notice card shows: photo (CDN URL in img src), full name, age calculated from date_of_birth, nationality flags, sex icon, charges from arrest_warrants, issuing countries, alarm badge
Cards update in-place on SSE events — never replace the img element
Nationality and country flags use Unicode Regional Indicator Symbol Letters (ISO-2 → flag emoji)
Images use object-fit: contain with a dark background container

features.description: Docker infrastructure — 5 services, compose, env config
done_criteria: docker-compose up --build runs without errors
All 5 services start: container-a, container-b, rabbitmq, postgres, minio
Healthchecks on rabbitmq and postgres; container-a and container-b depend_on them
All credentials and config use ${ENV_VAR} references only — no hardcoded values
All variables documented in .env.example with one-line descriptions
PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and MinIO data persisted with named volumes
README.md includes architecture overview and quick start (cp .env.example .env && docker-compose up --build)
Each container has its own requirements.txt
.env.example includes all variables with one-line descriptions
How to run tests is documented
Volume wipe requirement documented for credential changes (restart required when RabbitMQ or PostgreSQL credentials change)
